title: UPI and personal loans are replacing credit cards in Indians' wallets: CIBIL
summary: Credit card penetration in India remains low, with only 5.2 crore holders, representing 25% of the credit-active population. While usage is rising, first-time borrowers account for just 8% of new credit card issuances

title: PPF account turns dormant if you invest less than ₹500 a year: What you lose and how to revive it
summary: PPF accounts become inactive if the annual ₹500 minimum deposit is missed, restricting benefits. Activation requires a request and payment of a penalty. Full details here.

title: Sebi reintroduces open-market share buybacks: How your gains will be taxed and what to consider before participating
summary: Sebi has notified rules for open-market share buybacks effective 1 August 2026, allowing companies to repurchase shares through stock exchanges. Here's how your capital gains will be taxed and what to consider before selling your shares via the open market.

title: How many mutual funds should you own? Experts explain the right portfolio mix
summary: Managing too many mutual funds complicates portfolio tracking without enhancing diversification. Experts suggest three to six funds can suffice, emphasising a mix of large, mid, and small-cap funds along with debt for balance.&nbsp;

title: As the Magnificent 7 lose swagger, which stock should you buy now? Experts reveal the right strategy
summary: The Magnificent Seven tech giants are losing investor interest as their performance lags behind the Nasdaq 100 and S&amp;P 500. Emerging chipmakers like Micron Technology are gaining traction, while stocks like Alphabet show potential amidst differing fortunes among the group.

title: Quant Mid Cap Fund beats benchmark in short term after years of lag: Expert explains what investors should do
summary: Quant Mid Cap Fund has outperformed its benchmark index over the last 3 and 6 months, but continues to lag over the 1-, 3-, and 5-year periods. The expert attributed the rebound to portfolio repositioning and advised investors to focus on long-term consistency.&nbsp;

title: ITR filing 2026: Preventive health checkups can help you save tax — here's how
summary: The ITR filing deadline is approaching, prompting taxpayers to prepare documents and explore deductions. Section 80D allows deductions for preventive health checkups, capped at ₹5,000, applicable only in the old tax regime.

title: EPFO portal revamp: 10 changes in PF claims, withdrawals and transfers every member should know
summary: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation has centralised its member database through the CITES project to enhance service speed, transparency, and accessibility.&nbsp;

title: Can tenants continue paying old rent if they overstay after lease expires? Delhi HC answers
summary: The Delhi HC ruled that tenants staying in a property after lease expiration may face higher rent due to automatic escalation clauses. Here's all you need to know.

title: How much should you invest in mutual funds? Experts say there’s no magic number
summary: A 20-30% investment target can be a useful starting point, but the right SIP depends on your goals, emergency savings and ability to increase investments as income grows.

title: US stocks today: Dow falls 500 points after Trump says Iran deal is 'over'
summary: U.S. stocks closed lower after Donald Trump declared the Iran deal “over,” raising fears of further conflict and pushing oil prices higher. The Dow dropped sharply while the S&P 500 slipped. Chip stocks gained on Apple’s Broadcom deal, but inflation concerns and IMF growth cuts weighed on sentim

title: US SEC removes legal obstacle to UBS' crisis-resolution plan
summary: The U.S. SEC cleared a key legal hurdle for UBS by allowing potential debt-to-equity conversions under its crisis-resolution plan without enforcement action. The move supports “bail-in” mechanisms directed by Swiss regulators and addresses cross-border issues exposed during the Credit Suisse rescue,

title: Some US Fed officials considered rate hike on Iran war fallout, June meeting minutes reveal
summary: US central bank officials discussed rising inflation at their recent meeting. Most participants saw inflation falling on its own, but some favored immediate rate hikes. The committee ultimately decided to keep interest rates steady for now. Policymakers also considered shortening the policy statemen

summary: Despite a generally positive long-term outlook on gold, brokerages are adjusting their near-term expectations downward. title: Palantir shares fall 4% as AI software stocks come under pressure
summary: Palantir shares fell over 4% as investors sold high-valuation AI software stocks amid concerns over slower-than-expected AI adoption. Weak broader markets, insider selling, and bearish bets by Michael Burry added to pressure, signalling a wider rotation away from expensive technology stocks lacking

title: SBI Funds Management IPO to open on July 14. Check details
summary: SBI Funds Management has filed its red herring prospectus for an IPO comprising only an offer for sale by SBI and Amundi India Holding. The listing will offer investors exposure to India’s fast-growing mutual fund industry without raising fresh capital.

title: Market Trading Guide: Ather Energy among 2 stock recommendations for Thursday
summary: Indian markets declined sharply as rising crude prices, higher bond yields and renewed West Asia tensions hurt sentiment. Technical analysts recommended Alkyl Amines Chemicals and Ather Energy, citing bullish trends, strong momentum and favourable technical indicators.

title: Broadcom shares jump over 2% after Apple announces $30 billion chip deal
summary: Broadcom shares gained after Apple announced a multi-year chip supply agreement exceeding $30 billion. The deal expands US manufacturing, strengthens Broadcom’s custom silicon business through 2031 and supports Apple’s strategy to build a domestic semiconductor supply chain.

title: US stocks today: Dow Jones crashes 500 points at open as Trump stops Iran peace deal
summary: Wall Street opened lower after Trump said the Iran deal was over, triggering a risk-off sentiment globally. Rising oil prices added pressure on equities, with Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipping at the open as investors reacted to geopolitical uncertainty and potential inflation risks.
